name: repo-doctor
description: Legacy Codex plugin distribution for Repo Doctor Skills.

interface.shortDescription: Understand, review, diagnose, and safely change software repositories.
interface.longDescription: Repo Doctor is the legacy Codex plugin distribution for Repo Doctor Skills. It supports repository understanding, requirements, diagnosis, review, impact analysis, safe planning, focused fixes, test implementation, CI diagnosis, documentation synchronization, release readiness gates, and specialized engineering risk reviews.
defaultPrompt: Use Repo Doctor to understand this repository before making changes.
Use Repo Doctor to run a project health check without modifying code.
Use Repo Doctor to review my current changes with P0/P1/P2/P3 priorities.
Use Repo Doctor to analyze the impact of changing a specific file or module before editing.
Use Repo Doctor to turn an ambiguous request into a testable specification without modifying code.
Use Repo Doctor to reproduce a bug and establish an evidence-backed root cause without fixing it.
Use Repo Doctor to create an atomic, verifiable, rollback-ready change plan without executing it.
Use Repo Doctor to map a requirement or diff to existing tests and identify prioritized test gaps.
Use Repo Doctor to add the smallest high-value regression test without modifying production code.
Use Repo Doctor to diagnose the first trustworthy CI failure without editing files or exposing secrets.
Use Repo Doctor to synchronize documentation with a confirmed implementation while changing docs only.
Use Repo Doctor to evaluate a specific release candidate and return GO, GO WITH CONDITIONS, or NO-GO.
Use Repo Doctor to analyze a dependency upgrade without changing manifests or lockfiles.
Use Repo Doctor to review API contract compatibility across schemas, implementations, clients, docs, and tests.
Use Repo Doctor to review a database migration for reversibility, data safety, rollout order, and rollback.
Use Repo Doctor to verify whether suspected dead code is safe to remove without deleting it.
Use Repo Doctor to perform an evidence-backed security-focused review without attacking systems or applying fixes.
Use Repo Doctor to analyze a measured performance regression without directly optimizing code.
Use Repo Doctor to create or update an ADR while changing architecture documentation only.
Use Repo Doctor to audit configuration sources and drift without displaying credential values or modifying configuration.
Use Repo Doctor to safely fix the highest-priority issue from a previous project health check.
Use Repo Doctor to implement the smallest safe P0/P1 fix and validate the result.
